verses.commentary: The main point: We have such a high priest, who sat down at the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in heaven. This summarizes the argument. Christ is enthroned, not merely serving. His work is complete; His position is exalted.
Christ serves in the true sanctuary, of which the earthly was only a copy.

Every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices, and so it was necessary for this one also to have something to offer. Priesthood requires sacrifice. Christ offered Himself—the ultimate and final sacrifice.
He could not serve in the earthly temple. His priesthood is heavenly, not earthly.

They serve at a sanctuary that is a copy and shadow of what is in heaven. Moses was warned to make everything according to the pattern shown on the mountain. Exodus 25:40 cited. Earthly worship was always meant to be typological, pointing to heavenly reality.

But the ministry Jesus has received is as superior to theirs as the covenant of which He is mediator is superior to the old one, since the new covenant is established on better promises. Superior priesthood mediates superior covenant based on superior promises. Everything about the new is better.
It was never meant to be final.

But God found fault with the people and said: The days are coming when I will make a new covenant with Israel and Judah. Jeremiah 31:31-34 quoted. The problem was not the covenant itself but the people's inability to keep it. Yet God promises a new covenant that will overcome this failure.

It will not be like the covenant I made with their ancestors when I led them out of Egypt, because they did not remain faithful to my covenant, and I turned away from them. The Mosaic covenant was breakable; the people broke it. The new covenant will be different in nature, not just terms.

This is the covenant I will establish: I will put My laws in their minds and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be My people. Internalization replaces externalization. The law written on stone tablets becomes law written on hearts. Obedience flows from transformed nature, not external compulsion.

No longer will they teach their neighbor, saying, Know the Lord, because they will all know Me, from the least to the greatest. Universal knowledge of God among covenant members. No mediation required; direct relationship is the norm. This is fulfilled initially in the church and ultimately in the New Jerusalem.
God chooses not to remember sins. This is not amnesia but gracious choice to no longer hold sins against us.
Obsolete (palaiomenon) means worn out, ready to vanish. When Hebrews was written, the temple still stood. Soon it would be destroyed (AD 70), symbolically ending the old covenant era.
